concerns.text: FR-005 mandates a 'permutation test' to validate effects, while User Story 3 (US-3) and SC-001 explicitly define the validation method as a 'likelihood-ratio test' on a linear mixed-effects model. These are distinct statistical procedures with different null hypotheses and computational requirements. The spec contradicts itself on the primary validation mechanism for the interaction effect.

User Story 3 describes the analysis as fitting a linear mixed-effects model and performing a likelihood-ratio test. However, FR-005 introduces a new requirement for a permutation test on adaptation labels. The spec fails to clarify if the permutation test is an *additional* robustness check (which would require a new SC) or if it is intended to *replace* the likelihood-ratio test mentioned in US-3. This creates ambiguity in the acceptance criteria for the primary hypothesis.

The 'AdaptationLabel' entity is defined as a 'binary flag (Adaptive/Control) assigned to each passage'. However, the methodology (US-2) and FR-003 describe the adaptation as occurring at the 'window' level (2-second slices) based on real-time CLI. A passage may contain multiple windows with mixed CLI states. The entity definition conflicts with the operational logic of the system, which assigns adaptation status dynamically per window, not statically per passage.
